meta.name: Village Knowledge Expansion
meta.description: Curriculum design, lesson architecture, assessment creation, and teaching material development following ADDIE model and backward design.

tasks.title: Write 5 Measurable Learning Outcomes for One Module
tasks.subject: Learning Outcomes
tasks.successCriteria: 5 outcomes using measurable verbs (Bloom's Taxonomy) that match module scope

steps.action: Write the module's overall goal in 1-2 sentences (3 min)
Identify the learner transformation: what can they DO after this module? (5 min)
Review Bloom's Taxonomy verb lists for each cognitive level (2 min)
Draft 5 outcome statements using different verbs (remember, understand, apply, analyze, evaluate, create) (15 min)
Remove vague words: 'know', 'understand', 'learn' → replace with measurable actions (5 min)
Check that each outcome matches a specific lesson or activity in your module (5 min)
Save outcomes and add to your course design document (3 min)
Share outcomes with a peer for clarity check (2 min)

tasks.notes: Start with outcomes before designing content. Every activity and assessment should map to an outcome.

tasks.title: Design Complete Lesson Flow with Timing
tasks.subject: Lesson Architecture
tasks.successCriteria: Complete lesson flow with hook, teach, practice, reflect, and timing for each section
tasks.failureCondition: Lesson lacks structure or realistic timing
tasks.recoveryMission: Write a 4-step flow outline without timing

steps.action: Write the lesson objective based on your learning outcomes (3 min)
Design a hook: question, story, scenario, or problem to grab attention (5 min)
Outline 3 main teaching sections, each with a clear sub-objective (10 min)
Add a practice activity for each teaching section (10 min)
Design a reflection or exit ticket to check understanding (5 min)
Assign realistic time estimates to each section (total = lesson length) (10 min)
Review for pacing: too much content? not enough practice? (10 min)
Create a materials list for everything needed (3 min)
Save lesson plan in your curriculum folder (2 min)

tasks.notes: A 60-minute lesson typically has: 5 min hook, 30 min teaching (3x10 min), 15 min practice, 10 min reflection.

tasks.title: Build One Mastery Check Assessment
tasks.subject: Assessments
tasks.successCriteria: Assessment that directly measures a learning outcome with clear success criteria
tasks.failureCondition: Assessment cannot determine if outcome was achieved

steps.action: Select one learning outcome to assess (2 min)
Define what 'mastery' looks like for this outcome (5 min)
Choose assessment type: performance task, quiz, project, or demonstration (2 min)
Write clear instructions that a learner can follow independently (10 min)
Create the assessment task or questions (15 min)
Create an answer key or scoring guide (5 min)
Test the assessment yourself or with a colleague (4 min)
Revise based on testing feedback (2 min)

tasks.notes: A good mastery check takes 5-10 minutes to complete. It should clearly show who has mastered the outcome and who needs more support.

tasks.title: Build a Rubric for One Assessment
tasks.subject: Rubrics
tasks.successCriteria: Rubric with 3-5 criteria and 3-4 performance levels with clear descriptors

steps.action: List the skills or knowledge the assessment measures (5 min)
Turn each skill into a rubric criterion (e.g., 'Use of evidence') (5 min)
Define 3-4 performance levels (e.g., Exemplary, Proficient, Developing, Beginning) (3 min)
Write a descriptor for each criterion at each level (20 min)
steps.measurable: All cells filled (e.g., 4 criteria x 4 levels = 16 descriptors)

steps.action: Add concrete examples of what each level looks like (5 min)
Review for clarity and eliminate subjective language (5 min)
Save rubric with the assessment (2 min)

tasks.notes: Good rubrics make grading faster and more consistent. Share rubrics with learners before they start the assessment.
